entry.title: 4 Teams That Could Shape The MLB Trade Deadline
entry.summary:
Who should buy, who should sell, and who should stand pat at the MLB Trade Deadline? Alanna Rizzo, Todd Frazier and Erik Kratz play a game of "Wheeling and Dealing," breaking down the Mets, Blue Jays, Padres and Tigers while debating the biggest decisions each team faces before the deadline. Plus, the crew discusses potential trade targets, whether Tarik Skubal should be moved, and what could shape the market over the next two weeks.

entry.title: Why Today’s Athletes Say $100M Isn’t Enough | Bomani Jones & Domonique Foxworth

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones and Dominique Foxworth break down the growing $100 million contract debate and why more athletes are starting to question what that number really means today. As salaries keep rising and expectations keep changing, $100M might sound massive to fans, but for top players, it may not go as far as people think. . . . entry.title: Phillies Trade Deadline Plans Just Changed
entry.summary:
The Phillies suffered a major blow with the loss of Brad Keller for the rest of the season, creating an even bigger need ahead of the MLB Trade Deadline. Alanna Rizzo, Todd Frazier and Erik Kratz discuss how Keller's injury changes Dave Dombrowski's deadline priorities, whether Philadelphia should prioritize bullpen help over a right-handed bat, and which trade targets could help the Phillies make another postseason run.

entry.title: We Investigated (and Interviewed) Epstein's Billionaire Funder | PTFO
entry.summary:
Victoria's Secret mogul turned mega-donor Leslie Wexner introduced Jeffrey Epstein to Harvard. Now, a growing protest movement to strip his name from university buildings is getting shut down. What did Epstein's money man know? When did he know it? And who really calls the shots in higher education? The Harvard Crimson's Dhruv Patel returns for Part 3 in our series on America's most prestigious school and most notorious predator, to examine Wexner and Epstein's "friendship," plus an exclusive interview and a new tranche of internal fundraising transactions that reveals $100 million in publicly unaccounted donations.
• Part 1: WTF Is the Epstein Fund for Women's Sports? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1JclWYNwHQ
• Part 2: New Files Reveal How Epstein Paid Harvard to Launder His Image https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lmOHrXq2jpM
(Pablo Torre Finds Out is independently produced by Meadowlark Media and distributed by The Athletic. The views, research and reporting expressed in this episode are solely those of Pablo Torre Finds Out and The Harvard Crimson and do not reflect the work or editorial input of The Athletic or its journalists.)

entry.title: NOTHING has Changed with the Caitlin Clark Experience

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Howard Bryant to break down why the Caitlin Clark discourse keeps getting louder, dumber, and more political. Bo and Howard talk about Clark becoming a culture-war avatar, why the WNBA was always going to push back, whether the noise around her is distorting how people talk about her actual game, and why the Larry Bird comparisons still do not really add up. . . . entry.title: Messi Stops Time, Is $100M Not Enough? | Bomani Jones & Domonique Foxworth

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Domonique Foxworth for a loaded episode of The Right Time, starting with Lionel Messi and why it feels like the whole game stops when he touches the ball. Bo and Domonique break down Argentina’s late-game magic, why England’s approach fell apart, and how Messi’s gravity bends a match even when he is not dominating every possession. Later, they dive into the athlete-money discourse and explain what players are really trying to say when they claim $100 million does not go as far as fans think.
They talk about why that message always sounds out of touch, how young athletes can burn through huge contracts faster than people realize, why the real issue is lifestyle and financial literacy rather than sympathy, and how the whole conversation connects to athlete podcasts, boss culture, and Jay-Z’s version of capitalism.
. . . Subscribe to Supercast for Ad-Free Episodes: https://righttime.supercast.com/

entry.title: XPeng L03 DRIVEN: Has China SOLVED The Model Y?!
entry.summary:
The XPeng L03 is a $21,000 electric crossover with Model Y-rivalling space, advanced technology, and XPeng’s VLA 2.0 driving system. It costs around $18,000 less than the cheapest Tesla Model Y in China, and it is coming to Europe and other global markets.
XPeng L03 First Look: https://youtu.be/t9Oj4YOhW8A
In this video, I test the XPeng L03 on the road and examine its price, range, charging, interior, practicality, suspension, export specifications, and VLA 2.0 technology. With European pricing yet to be announced, could this half-price Model Y rival expose just how expensive mainstream EVs have become?
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇
____________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
- XPeng L03 starts from $18,000 less than Tesla Model Y in China - Uses 2 XPeng Turing AI Chips 1500 TOPs - XPeng VLA 2.0 ADAS (FSD Beta Rival) - Coming to Australia, Europe and 63 countries - XPeng VLA 2.0 coming to Europe in 2027 ____________________________________________________________

entry.title: How Aggressive Should The White Sox Be At The Trade Deadline?
entry.summary:
James Fegan of Sox Machine joins Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun following the announcement that Sox Machine is officially joining the Foul Territory Network! We discuss the surprising first-place White Sox, why Chicago's turnaround happened sooner than expected, and what the club should do at the trade deadline. Plus, James breaks down the White Sox's biggest roster needs, whether Reid Detmers is a realistic trade target, and which young players could become the foundation of the franchise for years to come.

entry.title: MLB Insider Robert Murray Shares The Latest Trade Deadline Buzz
entry.summary:
MLB insider Robert Murray of FanSided joins Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun to break down the latest trade deadline buzz around baseball. We discuss the Padres' plans with Mason Miller, the Brewers' trade for Lance McCullers Jr., which teams could become the biggest buyers and sellers, and why Tarik Skubal could still be the biggest name to watch before the deadline. Plus, Robert shares the latest on the Phillies' trade targets, the White Sox's surprising position as buyers, and a few under-the-radar players who could end up on the move.

entry.title: BREAKING: Reds Lock Up Chase Burns Long-Term
entry.summary:
Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Scott Braun break down Chase Burns' seven-year extension with the Reds and what it means for both sides. We discuss why more young pitchers are choosing long-term security over free agency, how Cincinnati has built one of baseball's best young rotations, and why the organization's investment in pitching development is paying off. Plus, we explain why this deal could become a blueprint for small-market teams moving forward.

entry.title: Decoding the Kawhi Leonard Investigation, with DeMaurice Smith | PTFO
entry.summary:
A superstar held up by the salary cap. A billionaire under the microscope. A league inquiry hidden from public view. De Smith has seen this playbook for punishment before. The former NFL union boss — and white-shoe law-firm investigator — returns to answer questions the NBA still won't: Who is "independent" when the league (and Steve Ballmer, in particular) pays the legal bill? Why would witnesses participate if they face potential criminal exposure? How do unions play the long game? And what obligation does a league have to its fans, if cheating can be the baseline?
• Read "Turf Wars" by DeMaurice Smith: https://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/735685/turf-wars-by-demaurice-smith/
• Binge-watch Pablo's Kawhi Leonard investigation: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLp43H6Vt_yDoUarxNb_fVK9_6bSiA2aHo
• Previously on PTFO — Digging Up NFL Collusion Dirt with Ex-Union Boss DeMaurice Smith: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=db4DXqq5iPY
(Pablo Torre Finds Out is independently produced by Meadowlark Media and distributed by The Athletic. The views, research and reporting expressed in this episode are solely those of Pablo Torre Finds Out and do not reflect the work or editorial input of The Athletic or its journalists.)

entry.title: These MLB Teams Changed Our Expectations For The Second Half
entry.summary:
A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun discuss the biggest surprises heading into the second half of the MLB season. They break down the Red Sox incredible turnaround, debate whether Boston should be buyers at the trade deadline, and explain what's gone wrong for the Athletics after their stunning collapse. Plus, the guys share their thoughts on the first-place White Sox, the Cubs playoff chances, and several under-the-radar teams that could make noise after the All-Star break.

entry.title: How the Celtics FAILED Brown & Tatum, Caitlin Clark Discourse Gets More Insane | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Howard Bryant for a loaded episode of The Right Time, starting with why the Boston Celtics failed the Jaylen Brown-Jayson Tatum era. Bo and Howard break down how Boston treated Brown like the expendable star, why the organization clearly picked Tatum as the face of the franchise, and what Tatum now has to prove coming off an Achilles injury. They also get into Brown’s move to Philadelphia, whether the basketball fit makes any sense next to Joel Embiid and Tyrese Maxey, and why the whole breakup felt colder than it needed to be.
Later, Bo and Howard dive into the Caitlin Clark discourse and explain why the conversation around her has somehow gotten even louder, dumber, and more political since she got to the WNBA. They talk about Clark becoming a culture-war avatar, why the league’s player-driven culture was always going to push back, whether her actual game is getting overrated by the noise around her, and why the Larry Bird comparisons don’t really hold up. . Subscribe to Supercast for Ad-Free Episodes: https://righttime.supercast.com/
Buy 'The Right Time' merch: http://therighttimebomani.com/

entry.title: Cole Hamels On The 2008 Phillies, Zack Wheeler & This Year's World Series Hopes
entry.summary:
Former MLB All-Star Cole Hamels joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from Philadelphia during MLB All-Star Week! Hamels reflects on the 2008 Phillies, explains why he still roots for the franchise, and shares behind-the-scenes stories from one of the greatest rotations in baseball. Plus, he weighs in on Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub, discusses how starting pitchers should be developed in today's game, and looks back on some unforgettable moments from his Phillies career.

entry.title: How Microtransactions (Almost) Ruined Gaming, with Dan Soder | PTFO
entry.summary:
Virtual currency is a new cheat code. But when EA Sports tried to squeeze extra cash out of college-football fans with so-called "microtransactions" inside the new CFB 27, gamers revolted... and won? Comedian Dan Soder, who's been playing video games with a future NFL coach since childhood, takes Pablo Torre on a journey to the seventh circle of nerddom: What does this phenomenon say about private equity turning fun into an asset? How do we stop sacrificing community for convenience? And is shame still possible?
• Subscribe to Dan Soder on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@DanSoder
Previously on PTFO:
• What It's Like to Play Madden Against an NFL Head Coach: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Drnz-oS6RIU
• We Smoked All the Athlete-Branded Weed We Could Find: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fZcahaB3hYI

entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from Philadelphia during MLB All-Star Week! Ken breaks down the new Home Run Derby format, explains why Philadelphia is the ideal host for the All-Star Game, and discusses what kind of reception Mike Trout could receive from the hometown crowd. Plus, he shares the latest trade deadline buzz surrounding the Tigers, Red Sox, Orioles and Yankees, and weighs in on Aaron Judge's injury.

entry.title: Ken Rosenthal On How MLB's Crowded Playoff Race Will Impact The Trade Deadline
entry.summary:
FT Senior Insider Ken Rosenthal breaks down how MLB's crowded playoff races could completely reshape this year's trade deadline. With 23 of 30 teams still within four games of a playoff spot entering the All-Star break, Ken explains why buyers and sellers are harder to identify than ever. Plus, he discusses what the Tigers should do with Tarik Skubal, why the Red Sox have changed the conversation, and how teams like the Astros, Blue Jays, Orioles, Diamondbacks and Padres could approach one of the most unpredictable deadlines in recent memory.

entry.title: Bam Adebayo punches Tyler Herro, Wembanyama's discount, Conor McGregor's next move | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is back from NBA Summer League in Las Vegas with a loaded episode of The Right Time, starting with the wildest story of the weekend: the Bam Adebayo-Tyler Herro fight.
From there, Bo gets into Victor Wembanyama’s five-year, $252 million extension with the San Antonio Spurs and explains why taking less than the full max could be great for the team but dangerous as a precedent for NBA stars. He also talks Summer League standouts, the AJ Dybantsa vs. Darryn Peterson matchup, the ongoing Kawhi Leonard and LA Clippers investigation & whether anyone actually cares how that case ends. He also reacts to Conor McGregor’s latest injury in Las Vegas, and Jaylen Brown’s recent comments.

entry.summary:
Braves ace Chris Sale joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from MLB All-Star Week in Philadelphia! Sale reflects on earning his 10th All-Star selection, looks back on his first Midsummer Classic alongside legends like Derek Jeter, Justin Verlander and David Ortiz, and discusses what it means to still be pitching at an elite level. Plus, Sale shares his thoughts on Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub, reveals whether he still has another gear on the mound, and tells hilarious stories from his early days with A.J. in Chicago.

entry.title: Max Muncy on His All-Star Experience, Ohtani's Knee Injury & The Dodgers Confidence
entry.summary:
Dodgers third baseman Max Muncy joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun live from Philadelphia during MLB All-Star Week! Muncy shares what it's like preparing for another All-Star Game, explains why he turned down the Home Run Derby, and reveals why he thinks one of the hometown Phillies has the edge to win it. Plus, he provides an update on Shohei Ohtani's knee, discusses the Dodgers confidence despite a rough finish before the break, and reflects on celebrating another World Series title with a White House visit.

entry.title: Vernon Maxwell GOES OFF on Celtics trading Jaylen Brown, Spurs Collapse vs. Knicks | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Vernon “Mad Max” Maxwell for a long, funny, and extremely candid NBA conversation on this episode of The Right Time. Maxwell returns to the show to talk about his second act in sports media, how he still trains like a player, and why he believes some of the biggest storylines in the league are being misunderstood.
Bomani and Max dive deep into the Jalen Brown conversation, including why Maxwell remains so high on Brown as a player, why he thinks the criticism has gone too far, and what Brown could look like in Philadelphia alongside Tyrese Maxey and Joel Embiid. They also get into what Boston may have gotten wrong, whether Jayson Tatum should have done more to keep Brown around, and why the Celtics’ decision still doesn’t sit right with Max.
The conversation also covers Hakeem Olajuwon’s one-of-one greatness, Giannis Antetokounmpo trade speculation involving the Miami Heat, LeBron James’ future after the Lakers, the appeal of a Jokic-LeBron fit in Denver, LaMelo Ball’s evolution, Jalen Brunson’s footwork and mentality, and why Victor Wembanyama could be on the verge of becoming the face of the NBA.
0:00 Intro 4:53 Why Vernon Maxwell puts Jalen Brown in his top five 9:32 Why moving on from Brown never made sense to 16:18 The real Sixers question 21:03 Giannis-to-Miami talk and what the Heat roster would still need 32:48 Where LeBron should go next 37:30 Spurs-Knicks Finals talk 42:21 Is this about to become Victor Wembanyama’s league?

entry.title: This NIO ES8 Has Fewer Seats And More EVERYTHING!
entry.summary:
The NIO ES8 5-seater takes NIO’s luxury electric SUV and makes it more spacious, more practical, and arguably more interesting. By removing the third row, NIO has created huge rear-seat space, a massive boot, and a five-seat version that feels like more than just a seat option.
NIO ES8 full Review: https://youtu.be/RNIcmEhxSgY
In this video, I look at what makes the NIO ES8 five-seat version different from the six-seat and seven-seat models, including the rear-seat layout, luxury features, cargo space, fridge, massage seats, and the latest NIO World Model ADAS update.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇
____________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
* NIO ES8 five-seat luxury electric SUV * Same 5.28m body as the six-seat and seven-seat ES8 * Huge second-row legroom with reclining rear bench * HUGE Trunk space * Latest NIO World Model ADAS update

entry.title: Anthony Volpe Shuts Down False Report From Michael Kay
entry.summary:
Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Alanna Rizzo react to Anthony Volpe shutting down a report that claimed he refused to play second base in the minors after Michael Kay publicly retracted the story. The trio discusses why a player's reputation matters, the responsibility that comes with anonymous sourcing, and why Volpe's response says a lot about the kind of teammate he is. Plus, they debate Don Mattingly's future with the Phillies and whether a deep postseason run should earn him the full-time manager's job.

entry.title: Why Dylan Cease Has Been Worth Every Penny For Toronto
entry.summary:
Erik Kratz, Kevin Pillar and Alanna Rizzo react to Dylan Cease falling just three outs shy of a no-hitter and debate whether the Blue Jays made the right decision letting him chase history. Erik, Kevin and Alanna discuss why Cease's durability, dominant stuff and the timing of the season made the decision an easy one, where he stands in the American League Cy Young race, and why Toronto's early investment in the right-hander is paying off. Plus, they examine how postseason workloads can influence managers' decisions when pitchers have a chance to make history.

entry.title: LeBron to Cleveland Again? Why Bomani Jones Isn’t Sold

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones & Danny Parkins break down the biggest LeBron landing spots and why a return to Cleveland, a reunion with Miami, or even a surprise move elsewhere all come with very different stakes.
This clip dives into whether LeBron still changes everything on a minimum deal, why the Harden fit in Cleveland feels shaky, and how teams like Miami, Golden State, San Antonio, Denver, and Minnesota all create fascinating what-if scenarios.

entry.title: Shohei Ohtani Is Building A One-Of-A-Kind Legacy
entry.summary: A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun react to Shohei Ohtani becoming the first Japanese-born player to reach 300 career home runs and discuss what the latest milestone means for his incredible legacy. The guys explain why Ohtani's combination of elite power and pitching makes his accomplishments unlike anything baseball has ever seen, debate whether he has a realistic shot at winning both MVP and Cy Young this season, and look ahead to whether 500 home runs is within reach. Plus, they compare Ohtani's long-term outlook to other sluggers and discuss how his career could evolve once he's done pitching.
entry.content: A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun react to Shohei Ohtani becoming the first Japanese-born player to reach 300 career home runs and discuss what the latest milestone means for his incredible legacy. The guys explain why Ohtani's combination of elite power and pitching makes his accomplishments unlike anything baseball has ever seen, debate whether he has a realistic shot at winning both MVP and Cy Young this season, and look ahead to whether 500 home runs is within reach. Plus, they compare Ohtani's long-term outlook to other sluggers and discuss how his career could evolve once he's done pitching.

entry.title: Jim Bowden Maps Out The MLB Trade Deadline For Every Team
entry.summary:
FT Resident GM Jim Bowden joins A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun for a division-by-division look at the MLB trade deadline. Bowden breaks down which contenders should buy, which teams should sell, and the biggest names to watch across both leagues, including his thoughts on the Dodgers, Mets, Reds, Mariners, Tigers and more. Plus, Jim sounds off on Zack Wheeler's All-Star snub, Bryce Harper's Legends selection, and why MLB needs to rethink the current All-Star selection process.

entry.title: A.J. Pierzynski & Erik Kratz React To Justin Verlander's Retirement
entry.summary:
A.J. Pierzynski, Erik Kratz and Scott Braun react to Justin Verlander's announcement that he'll retire following the 2026 season. A.J. and Erik share stories from competing against Verlander throughout their careers, explain how he transformed the modern power pitcher, and break down the adjustments that allowed him to reinvent himself multiple times, from his MVP season in Detroit to his Cy Young comeback after Tommy John surgery. Plus, the guys reflect on Verlander's legendary preparation, remarkable longevity, and why he's an unquestioned first-ballot Hall of Famer.

entry.title: USMNT DISASTER vs. Belgium, Donovan Mitchell's absurd contract, LeBron's next move | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Danny Parkins of First Things First. They start with Team USA’s loss to Belgium and what it says about the current state of American soccer. They break down the Balogun red-card drama, whether the U.S. benefited from a favorable draw, why this might still be the most talented team the country has ever put on the field, and whether the American youth soccer system is actually built to develop top-level players.
From there, they shift to the NBA and dig into the Lakers’ roster, Luka’s fit, Bronny’s possible role, and what kind of team Los Angeles is really trying to build. They also talk through the Celtics’ roster decisions, the changing math of paying star players, and the most fascinating possible next chapter for LeBron.

entry.title: Breaking Down The Mets Trade Deadline Decisions & Blue Jays Outlook
entry.summary:
A.J. Pierzynski and Erik Kratz break down two of baseball's most fascinating teams as the trade deadline approaches. The guys discuss why the Mets appear ready to move Freddy Peralta and other key pieces, what his recent changes on the mound could mean for his trade value, and whether New York should sell beyond expiring contracts. Plus, they examine the Blue Jays recent collapse, what's gone wrong after last year's World Series run, and whether Toronto still has enough time to get its season back on track.

entry.title: Why Jaylen Brown ACTUALLY got traded, LeBron James next move, Balogun returns for US | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones breaks down the Jaylen Brown trade, why the Boston Celtics decided to move on, and what the deal says about the NBA’s new era of team-building, player value, and trade season. On this episode of The Right Time, Bomani explains why Jaylen Brown remains a highly productive scorer while also unpacking the bigger debate around advanced stats, roster construction, and the never-ending “athletes vs. math elites” conversation.
Bomani also looks at what happens next for LeBron James, why his next move may be about more than basketball, and which teams make the most sense as the NBA keeps orbiting around his future.
Plus, Bomani gets into geopolitics at the World Cup, and a hilarious reaction to Trump supporters getting stuck inside the Smithsonian National Museum of African American History and Culture during a July 4 storm.

entry.title: Uncovering Phil Mickelson's Misconduct Allegations | PTFO
entry.summary:
After months of rumors, journalist Alan Shipnuck investigates why Phil Mickelson has basically vanished from the LIV Golf Tour — and social media. Leading us down a rabbit hole of hidden misconduct claims and exits from three different golf clubs. Not to mention 19 sources, an off-shore oil pipeline and an ex-friend's ex-wife: Ashley Perez.
Read Alan Shipnuck’s reporting at Skratch Golf: https://www.skratch.golf/news/pro-golf/phil-mickelson-misconduct-women-madison-club-bridges-farms-exits
Read Phil Mickelson’s spokesperson’s statement: https://x.com/_davidrumsey/status/2072655169792225420?s=46
Read Skratch’s response: https://x.com/_davidrumsey/status/2072696394997981241?s=46

entry.title: The Night Hulk Hogan Shocked the World and Joined the NWO | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Corey Erdman to break down Hulk Hogan’s legendary heel turn at Bash at the Beach 1996, when Hogan shocked fans, joined the NWO, and changed the future of WCW, WWE, and pro wrestling forever.
In this episode of The Right Time, Bomani and Corey revisit Hulkamania, Hollywood Hogan, the rise of the New World Order, and why Hogan’s jump to WCW initially felt like a legacy act before the NWO made him feel brand new again.
They also discuss why the turn hit so hard, how it helped reshape the Monday Night Wars and the Attitude Era, and why Hogan remained the most recognizable star wrestling ever produced.
Topics include Hulk Hogan, NWO, WCW, WWE, Bash at the Beach, Hollywood Hogan, Eric Bischoff, Kevin Nash, Scott Hall, the Monday Night Wars, and wrestling nostalgia.

entry.title: Xpeng L03: European Carmakers Will Panic Over This Chinese EV
entry.summary:
The Xpeng Mona L3 is coming for the European EV market. See why mainstream manufacturers are worried about this new competitor.
European car brands like VW and Audi are facing serious competition as premium Chinese EVs enter their home territory. The Xpeng Mona L3 represents a new strategy, bringing advanced, premium tech into the entry-level EV segment. This shift creates a direct challenge for established mainstream OEMs that have previously dominated the region.
In this video, we take a quick first look at the XPeng L03 entry-level EV and break down how XPeng is trying to bring premium-level tech into the mainstream EV segment. This car is clearly aimed at both China and global markets, including Europe.
The bigger picture here is what this car represents. Chinese EV brands are no longer just challenging European luxury cars, they are now targeting the mainstream market where brands like Ford, Nissan, and Renault operate.
Disclaimer: The vehicle shown in the video is the Chinese-spec model filmed in China. Models and configurations for other markets shall be subject to actual vehicles available locally.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ____________________________________________________________ ✅ Highlights:
- Entry-level EV with premium-level technology stack - 3 in-house developed self-driving chips (~2250 TOPS compute) - 800V architecture with fast charging capability - EV and EREV variants with up to 1000km+ combined range

entry.title: The Sporting Class: Not Even FIFA Can Mess This Up | PTFO
entry.summary:
Ex-ESPN president John Skipper was once the most important soccer fan in America. But then he lost the World Cup to FOX — and became a federal witness. So we wanted to know what it’s been like to watch America fall in love with the very tournament Skipper wasn’t allowed to win. And whether federal corruption trials, nine figures worth of commercials and a Trump Tower apartment full of cats can slow the spread of the world’s favorite game.
• Donate to the Kyra Fund, which will support research on Glioblastoma, one of the deadliest cancers: https://fundraise.gbmresearch.org/campaign/812889/donate

entry.title: Why Kevin Durant LEFT the Thunder for the Warriors 10 years ago | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
On this episode of The Right Time with Bomani Jones, Bomani is joined by Logan Murdock to revisit Kevin Durant joining the Golden State Warriors and why KD left the Oklahoma City Thunder for Golden State in 2016. Ten years later, they break down why Kevin Durant chose the Warriors, how the Warriors built the perfect pitch, and why the move became one of the biggest turning points in modern NBA history.
Bomani Jones and Logan Murdock discuss the 2016 NBA offseason, the salary cap spike that made Kevin Durant to the Warriors possible, the Bay Area tech boom, the Warriors culture, and why Golden State felt like the right fit for KD at that moment. They also get into how Steph Curry, Draymond Green, and the Warriors' homegrown core made the team so appealing, plus why the partnership eventually became more complicated behind the scenes.
If you're interested in Kevin Durant highlights, Kevin Durant Warriors history, OKC Thunder history, Golden State Warriors history, NBA free agency, NBA superteams, and basketball analysis from Bomani Jones, this episode breaks it all down.

entry.title: “Please Enjoy It Or You Are Fired” (feat. Dan Le Batard) | PTFO
entry.summary:
PTFO keeps winning awards. But what do we do for an encore? How do you make something special without losing our standards — or our minds? Do you guys even enjoy this? Pablo visits Miami to see his friend/boss, Dan Le Batard, and Dan’s producer, Chris Cote. They discuss balancing real life; perspective in the face of grief; Oprah; and figuring out how to serve an audience without missing the point.
• Donate to the Kyra Fund, which will support research on Glioblastoma, one of the deadliest cancers: https://fundraise.gbmresearch.org/campaign/812889/donate

entry.title: We Remember the World Cup's Missing Muckraker | PTFO
entry.summary:
He covered soccer before it was cool, promised to go WikiLeaks on FIFA, then got detained and mysteriously collapsed in Qatar. If there was ever someone we needed to hear from, as a global conduit during this corrupt World Cup on American soil, it's Grant Wahl. So his former co-host, Chris Wittyngham — along with his widow and one of the many stars he inspired — helps Pablo Torre listen back to Wahl's lost tapes, re-live his fearless run for office and the rainbow target on his chest… then put the conspiracies to rest.
Previously on PTFO:
• John Green & Daniel Alarcón on World Cup Fandom — and the Worst Messi Interview Ever https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BFgJm0aUhgk
• Tim Weah on World Cup Dreams, the African Diaspora and USMNT's Fair-Weather Knicks Fandom https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_B0SbKYbiKw
• Following the Wasted Money at World Cup 2026 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9J8fYQhfjE0
Plus:
• Watch Chris Wittyngham on CBS Sports Golazo https://www.youtube.com/@cbssportsgolazo
• Subscribe to The Athletic https://theathletic.onelink.me/eNRy/ptfo

entry.title: How LeBron BROKE the 73-Win Warriors in 2016 | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Ethan Strauss to look back at the 2016 NBA Finals and how LeBron James, Kyrie Irving and the Cleveland Cavaliers came back to beat Steph Curry and the 73-win Golden State Warriors.
On this episode of The Right Time, Bomani and Ethan break down what made LeBron’s performance so overwhelming, why Game 7 still feels like one of the biggest legacy games in NBA history, and how the 2016 Finals changed the way people looked at Steph Curry, the Warriors dynasty and the Cavs’ championship in Cleveland.
They discuss LeBron vs. Steph, Kyrie’s role in the comeback, Draymond Green, the Warriors’ collapse, Game 7’s defining plays, and why the 2016 NBA Finals still stands as one of the most important series of the modern era.
Topics include: LeBron James’ control of the 2016 NBA Finals Steph Curry’s legacy after the Warriors blew a 3-1 lead Kyrie Irving’s impact on the Cavs’ championship run The pressure on the 73-win Warriors The biggest moments from Cavs vs. Warriors Game Why Cleveland’s title hit different

entry.title: John Green & Daniel Alarcón on World Cup Fandom — and the Worst Messi Interview Ever | PTFO
entry.summary:
We are numb, as Americans, to the attention of the globe and the power structures of our sports leagues. But what if love of country could be simple again? And what if fans had more power than we thought? Pablo Torre feels the football osmosis from the (banned) bestselling author and his old friend turned MacArthur Genius, as they offer a liberal-arts education in the beautiful game — from the fault in our superstar interviews to the damage wrought by Alexi Lalas upon our would-be Neymars and Messis. Plus: a pump-up speech that Ted Lasso would never, ever fantasize about.
• Subscribe to "The Away End with Daniel Alarcón and John Green": https://www.youtube.com/@AwayEndPodcast
• Previously on PTFO: John Green on the Fault in Our Health Care — and AFC Wimbledon's Fairytale https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qHiKbkWnjgo
• Subscribe to The Athletic: https://theathletic.onelink.me/eNRy/ptfo

entry.title: How the 1986 NBA Draft Changed the NBA Forever | Bomani Jones & Howard Bryant

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones and Howard Bryant break down the 1986 NBA Draft and why it remains one of the most important turning points in NBA history. This Time Machine Tuesday episode covers Len Bias, Dennis Rodman, Brad Daugherty, Arvydas Sabonis, the Boston Celtics, the Los Angeles Lakers, and how the 1986 draft changed the league forever.
If you’re looking for NBA history, Len Bias analysis, 1986 NBA Draft context, or a deep dive into the Bird vs. Magic era, this episode goes all the way there. Bomani and Howard Bryant unpack the NBA’s rise in the 1980s, the tragedy of Len Bias, the fallout of the so-called cocaine draft, and why this draft reshaped the future of pro basketball.
Topics include: 1986 NBA Draft Len Bias and the Celtics Dennis Rodman’s draft class Bird, Magic, and the NBA boom Arvydas Sabonis and international basketball How the NBA changed in the late 1980s
#BomaniJones #HowardBryant #NBAHistory #NBADraft #LenBias #DennisRodman #Celtics #Lakers

entry.title: Sarah Jessica Parker on the Power of Sports and the City | PTFO
entry.summary:
New York is palpably horny, right now. And happy. So we invited the patron saint of sex and this town to chase euphoria, appreciate the romance of the American idea, advise these young Knicks, remember the halcyon days of HBO... and make the case for reading books in the crowd.

entry.title: Tim Weah on World Cup Dreams, the African Diaspora and USMNT's Fair-Weather Knicks Fandom | PTFO
entry.summary:
Timothy Weah is the realest member of Team USA — an activist athlete, from Brooklyn and the African diaspora, choosing to represent a divisive nation as it tries bringing the globe a little closer together. In a rare sit-down ahead of the U.S. men's national team match against Australia, he explains the pressure of this moment, the power of soccer over politics... and why the Knicks' good-luck charm might need to strike again.

entry.title: The Brendan Sorsby Case EXPOSES College Football’s Biggest Problem | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Joel Anderson for a wide-ranging conversation about the Brendan Sorsby gambling case, the NCAA’s response, and what it says about the future of college sports. Bomani and Joel break down why college football’s reaction to Sorsby felt so extreme, how the NCAA continues to struggle in court, and why the Protect College Sports Act could be a major problem for athletes.
They also discuss whether Sorsby could end up in the NFL Supplemental Draft, how teams might evaluate him, and why gambling addiction should be treated more seriously as sports betting becomes impossible to avoid. Later, Bomani and Joel get into the hypocrisy around punishing young athletes, and Joel’s ongoing battle with arguing on the internet.

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama’s heel turn, Jalen Brunson’s New York Takeover | Bomani Jones & Domonique Foxworth

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Dominique Foxworth for another episode of The Right Time, and they start with the massive NBA Finals ratings after the Knicks’ championship run. Bomani and Dominique discuss why New York’s title felt so big, how Jalen Brunson became a permanent New York legend, and why the biggest winner of the Knicks’ championship might actually be James Dolan.
Then, Bomani and Dominique get into the backlash around Victor Wembanyama after the Finals. Was Wemby really a “villain,” or are fans just looking for the next superstar to tear down? They break down the criticism of his physicality, the handshake discourse, and why the reaction to Wemby says a lot about how we talk about young stars.
Plus, Bomani shares what it was like watching Senegal vs. France in Harlem during the World Cup, the energy around soccer in New York City, and why the World Cup hits differently depending on which neighborhood you’re in.
Topics include: Knicks championship reaction, Jalen Brunson’s New York status, James Dolan’s redemption moment, Victor Wembanyama backlash, NBA Finals ratings, handshake discourse, World Cup energy in NYC, Senegal vs. France, and why the Nets may never catch the Knicks.
Chapters: 0:00 Intro & Dominique Foxworth joins the show 1:15 NBA Finals ratings boom and the Knicks effect 9:00 Victor Wembanyama's Finals performance gets reassessed 19:00 The Anthony Edwards handshake controversy 26:20 Jalen Brunson's rise to New York legend status 33:50 The biggest winner of the Knicks title: James Dolan 40:10 Did the Sphere accidentally save the Knicks? 47:45 Soccer crowds, fan culture, and demographics 52:00 What happens to the Nets now? 55:20 Outro . . . entry.title: Celebrating These Knicks Championship Vibes with Desus Nice
entry.summary:
Transcendence on the court. Dancing in the streets. And a drunk Instagram Live for the ages. Why wait 'til the great Knicks parade of 2026? Pablo raises a glass (while devouring an egg-and-cheese) with Desus — his fellow New Yorker and bard of Knicks nation — to the commute of a champion and the beauty of a bandwagon; to the drip of Landry Shamet and the sweat of Patrick Ewing; to KAT and OG and Wu-Tang and, yes, even Coldplay. Just not that traitor from Sesame Street.

entry.title: Maradona’s Hand of God, Zidane’s Headbutt & the World Cup’s Wildest Moments | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones is joined by Spencer Hall for a Time Machine Tuesday edition of The Right Time to revisit some of the most chaotic, controversial, and unforgettable WTF moments in World Cup history.
Bo and Spencer break down Diego Maradona’s infamous “Hand of God” goal against England in the 1986 World Cup, the geopolitical weight behind Argentina vs. England, and why Maradona followed one of soccer’s most blatant acts of cheating with one of the greatest goals the sport has ever seen.
Then, they jump to the 2006 World Cup Final and Zinedine Zidane’s stunning headbutt against Italy, discussing how trash talk, pressure, and Italian defensive dark arts helped create one of the most shocking exits in sports history.
Plus, Bo and Spencer look ahead to the 2026 World Cup in North America, from brutal summer heat to public transportation problems, stadium naming chaos, and why international fans may not be ready for how big — and inconvenient — the United States really is.

entry.title: Jalen Brunson, the Knicks, and the NBA Championship That Took Over New York | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
The Knicks finally did it.
Bomani Jones reacts to the New York Knicks winning the NBA championship, Jalen Brunson’s legendary 45-point closeout performance, and what this title means for New York City. Bo breaks down how Brunson cemented himself as the king of New York, why the Knicks’ depth, conditioning, and maturity ultimately separated them from the Spurs, and how Mike Brown coached a championship-level series.
Bomani also reflects on the unique relationship between the Knicks and New York City — from Madison Square Garden to the streets, bars, parks, and neighborhoods that turned this championship run into a citywide experience. Why does this title feel different from other championships? Why do the Knicks hit New Yorkers so deeply? And what does this moment say about basketball as theater, culture, and community?
Plus, Bomani discusses the start of the World Cup, the excitement around the U.S. men’s national team, and the experience of international fans discovering America through sports, road trips, Waffle House, Buc-ee’s, and the South.

entry.title: Wemby, De’Aaron Fox and the Spurs Blew an All-Time Lead vs. Knicks | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones reacts to one of the wildest NBA playoff endings in recent memory as the New York Knicks storm back against Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs. After the Spurs dominated the first half and looked ready to take control of the series, everything fell apart late — from rushed shots and questionable coaching decisions to De’Aaron Fox’s brutal second-half execution.
Bomani breaks down why this collapse reminded him of infamous football meltdowns like the Houston Oilers blowing a 35-3 lead and the Atlanta Falcons’ 28-3 Super Bowl disaster. He explains why San Antonio’s inability to slow the game down, manage the clock, and protect a massive lead turned a dominant performance into an all-time choke.
The conversation also gets into Wemby’s missed free throws, OG Anunoby’s incredible game-winning tip-in, Jalen Brunson’s late-game shot-making, Josh Hart nearly becoming the story for the wrong reason, and why Madison Square Garden’s nervous energy makes Knicks playoff games feel different. Bomani also questions whether the Knicks’ comeback was more about their resilience or San Antonio completely losing control.
Plus, Bomani discusses the NBA’s ongoing officiating discourse, why ref talk takes over every playoff conversation, how social media fuels conspiracy theories, and what to expect in Game 5 as the Spurs try to avoid letting one loss beat them twice.

entry.title: Following the Wasted Money at World Cup 2026 | PTFO
entry.summary:
The 2026 World Cup is underway, and FIFA stands to profit nearly $9 billion. How did U.S. taxpayers get stuck with the tab? The Athletic's Adam Crafton crunches the numbers on wasted public money, as Pablo Torre dissects comically absurd demands upon host cities — from "clean" stadiums and super-V.I.P. treatment... to a pop-up jail.

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama's Villain Arc + Knicks have a HUGE problem in the NBA Finals | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
On this episode of The Right Time, Bomani Jones is joined by Tom Haberstroh to break down why Victor Wembanyama has completely changed this series and why the New York Knicks suddenly look like they’re running out of answers. They dig into Wemby’s dominance on both ends, how San Antonio keeps getting him downhill and at the rim, and why Tom thinks the Spurs should actually feel like they could be up 3-0 right now.
Bomani and Tom also get into what’s going wrong for the Knicks late in games, including Jalen Brunson’s extreme ball dominance, the lack of flow in New York’s offense, and Karl-Anthony Towns disappearing in crunch time after such a strong start to the series. From there, they discuss whether the Knicks can make the adjustments needed in Game 4, whether Wemby has already become the biggest force in the series, and why his physical edge and swagger are turning him into one of the most polarizing stars in the league.
They also hit the controversial officiating around Wemby’s contact with Brunson, the NBA’s response to that play, what this matchup says about who’s built for the moment, and even where Jalen Brunson belongs in the all-time Knicks conversation.

entry.title: Jeremy Lin Opens Up on Knicks 2026 Finals Run & the Shadow of Linsanity
entry.summary:
Madison Square Garden hasn't felt this alive since a little-known point guard from Harvard became the most popular person in New York City. On the occasion of his grand return, Jeremy Lin sits down with Pablo Torre, who's been covering him since their dorm-room days, for a full-circle conversation about pressure, toughness, fleeting fame, the end of his run with the Knicks, squashing the beef with Carmelo Anthony and, yes, getting recruited by Kim Kardashian.

entry.title: Victor Wembanyama Has the Knicks SHOOK + Brendan Sorsby’s NCAA Gambling Case | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones reacts to Victor Wembanyama and the Spurs flipping the NBA Finals with a physical Game 3 win over the Knicks, including why New York looked rattled, why Jalen Brunson got baited into ISO ball, and why Wemby set the tone in a hostile environment.
Later, Bomani breaks down Brendan Sorsby’s NCAA gambling case, the temporary injunction that let him play this season, and why the bigger story is about zero-tolerance policies, addiction, and the NCAA’s athlete-first standard.
Plus, Bomani gets to a few voicemails and stories on Ray Allen, Scott Skiles, and White Keisha.

entry.title: Knicks come home for game 3, Victor Wembanyama Backlash, World Cup Fever heats up | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones breaks down why the New York Knicks suddenly look like a completely different team in the NBA Finals, why they feel inevitable with a 2-0 lead, and why this run is starting to feel a lot bigger than anyone expected.
Bo also gives Karl-Anthony Towns his proper credit, arguing that KAT has been the best player on the floor, while pushing back on the easy Victor Wembanyama criticism by explaining how much of his rough night was really about the Knicks’ size, physicality, and defense.
From there, Bo and Ryan get into the huge NBA Finals ratings bump, what Victor’s star power means for the league, why the World Cup’s dangerous heat is becoming one of the tournament’s biggest storylines, and why Anthropic’s warning about powerful AI sounds a lot less crazy than people want to admit.

From the Spurs’ late-game shot selection to the bigger problem with how they’re using Wemby, Bomani explains why this was about more than one bad play and why the internet was waiting to pile on.

entry.title: "This Is Mind-Blowing": Steve Ballmer's Aspiration I.O.U., Uncovered | PTFO
entry.summary:
As Adam Silver calls for the NBA's Kawhi Leonard probe to wrap up and Aspiration co-founder Joe Sanberg gets 14 years behind bars, Pablo Torre investigates how Clippers owner Steve Ballmer covered his tracks on a side deal of his own — and with the SEC on the case. Former DOJ prosecutor Lou Manzo returns to make sense of the relationship between a billionaire and a fraudster, while David Samson is finally convinced that the richest owner in sports has lost credibility.
(Additional reporting by Anita Bennett)
• Take the PTFO audience survey: https://forms.gle/i2K6FecAbvBqDxRK9

(Pablo Torre Finds Out is independently produced by Meadowlark Media and distributed by The Athletic. The views, research and reporting expressed in this episode are solely those of Pablo Torre Finds Out and do not reflect the work or editorial input of The Athletic or its journalists.)

entry.title: Knicks DOMINATE Spurs in game 1 of NBA Finals, Russell Wilson's Hall of Fame case | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
In this episode of The Right Time, Bomani Jones and Danny Parkins break down why Karl-Anthony Towns might be the real key to a New York Knicks title run.
Bomani argues that KAT has been the Knicks' best player in the postseason and explains why New York looks its most dangerous when the offense runs through Towns instead of falling into heavy Jalen Brunson isolation ball.
They also get into how Towns' aggressiveness has changed the way people see him, why his effort level has been the biggest difference, and what this kind of playoff run could mean for his long-term legacy if the Knicks finish the job.
Later in the episode, Bomani and Danny debate Russell Wilson's Hall of Fame case, including how much his Pro Bowls matter, how his resume compares to Matthew Stafford, and whether public opinion has swung too far against him after the Denver years.

entry.title: Ahmed intros Baby Rogers + QB Countdown recap | Chris Simms Unbuttoned (FULL Ep. 884) | NFL on NBC
entry.summary:
“My boyfriend’s back and he’s better than ever…” Chris and Ahmed reunite to recap the Simms QB Countdown and answer some #AskMeAnything questions. Who would Chris rather have right now, Drake London or Nico Collins? But first, we welcome the newest member of the Unbuttoned family…Cameron Rogers! #NFLonNBC #NFL #ChrisSimmsUnbuttoned
Chapters: (0:00) We proudly present…Cameron Rogers!!! Who already has his dad’s style. (6:20) News: Nick Herbig gets big contract (11:10) Micah Parsons will miss the start of the season (17:25) Drake London gets 4-year extension (23:25) Rome Odunze’s eye-opening comments on his foot (25:10) Russell Wilson retires (32:25) QB Countdown: Which Draft Class quartet would Chris take for the future? (37:50) Homies react to Chris’ Top 10 (38:55) QB #7 Justin Herbert…is he given too many excuses? (42:05) QB #6 Caleb Williams…could he be #1 next year? (47:40) QB #5 Matthew Stafford…should he have been Top 4? (50:20) QB #4 Patrick Mahomes…do his accomplishments make him overrated or underrated? (53:10) QB #3 Lamar Jackson & #2 Joe Burrow…what separates them? (55:15) QB #1 Josh Allen…is he truly “unstoppable?” (1:03:35) #AskMeAnything: Myles Garrett MVP? Super Kicker? And when is Chris gonna drop another song?

entry.title: Hot on the Trail of Jeffrey Epstein with Reporter Julie K. Brown | PTFO
entry.summary:
Julie K. Brown went from working in a bell factory to ringing the alarm on masters of the universe and (finally) winning a Pulitzer Prize. The Miami Herald investigative reporter visits Pablo Torre to shed light on the culture of silence that protected Jeffrey Epstein for so long, the legal dream team that struck his secret deal, then how Epstein pulled out all the stops... to stop her reporting. Plus: the whistleblower who gave away the A-Rod steroid scandal, sneaking into a hospital to interview Joe Frazier... and the maddening therapy of Philly sports.
• Take the PTFO audience survey: https://forms.gle/i2K6FecAbvBqDxRK9
• Read "Perversion of Justice" by Julie K. Brown: https://www.harpercollins.com/products/perversion-of-justice-julie-k-brown?variant=44010796908578
Previously on PTFO:
• Part 1: Investigating Harvard's Hidden Epstein Files — WTF Is the Epstein Fund for Women's Sports? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V1JclWYNwHQ
• Part 2: New Files Reveal How Epstein Paid Harvard to Launder His Image https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lmOHrXq2jpM
• We Showed a Basketball Icon His Name in the Epstein Files. Things Got Awkward. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Le4j0Goj1NI

entry.title: Wemby arrives at the NBA Finals, Jay-Z vs. Drake, A.J. Brown & Patriots pressure | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones and Jason Goff break down the NBA Finals matchup between the Spurs and Knicks, with a deep dive into Victor Wembanyama, Jalen Brunson, Karl-Anthony Towns, and the biggest storylines shaping this series. From Wemby’s rise on the biggest stage in basketball to the pressure on Brunson and KAT to deliver in the Finals, this episode covers what matters most heading into a massive championship matchup.
They also get into whether Victor Wembanyama is on a path we haven’t seen since LeBron, why the Knicks’ guards could decide the series, and why these Finals feel loaded with legacy-defining stakes. Beyond the NBA Finals talk, Bomani and Jason also discuss the possibility of a Jay-Z vs. Drake battle, why that rap beef feels unwinnable for Drake, what A.J. Brown could mean for the Patriots, and how much pressure could be coming with that move.

entry.title: Why Dirk Nowitzki’s 2011 Title Run Changed Everything about his Legacy | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones and Vinnie Goodwill go back to the two NBA Finals that defined Dirk Nowitzki’s career: 2006 and 2011.
They break down how Dirk changed the league, why the “soft” label followed him for years, how the We Believe Warriors loss stuck to him, and why that 2011 championship run completely changed the way we talk about his legacy.
If you remember Dwyane Wade living at the line, the fake cough, the Heatles pressure cooker, and Dallas finally getting its revenge, this is the full Dirk story.
#TheRightTime #BomaniJones #DirkNowitzki #NBA

entry.title: Wemby leads Spurs to Finals, OKC's Chet Holmgren problem, NBA's Tanking fix | Bomani Jones

author.name: The Right Time with Bomani Jones

entry.summary:
Bomani Jones reacts to Spurs vs. Thunder Game 7 and explains why Victor Wembanyama is turning neutral fans into Spurs fans. On this episode of The Right Time, Bo breaks down Wemby vs. Chet Holmgren, what made the San Antonio Spurs so compelling, and why the Oklahoma City Thunder are still better than a lot of people want to admit.
Then Bomani dives into the NBA’s new draft lottery reform and what it means for tanking, team-building, and why the league is trying to stop late-season embarrassment. After that, Bo gets into the New York Giants drama surrounding Jaxson Dart, Abdul Carter, Donald Trump, and Jameis Winston’s completely chaotic comments.
00:00 Introduction 00:45 Spurs Beat Thunder in game 7 16:10 Chet gets owned by Victor Wemanyama 37:55 NBA Lottery gets changed 43:15 Jaxson Dart speaks on introducing Trump

entry.title: Sixers Hire Mike Gansey, Maxey and Embiid Grades, NBA Lottery Reform with Kevin O'Connor

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
The Sixers hired their newest President of Basketball Operations, Mike Gansey (on the 8th anniversary of Burnergate). We talked about the hire and what it means for the team going forward. Then we give grades to Joel Embiid and Tyrese Maxey for the 25-26 season. Then Yahoo's Kevin O'Connor joins us to talk about Gansey, and explain and discuss the NBA's Lottery reform measures.
KOC: 48:35

entry.title: We Unearth Shocking New Harambe Video, 10 Years Later | PTFO
entry.summary:
Ten years ago, Harambe's death broke the internet, from sports and politics to million-dollar memes. But this viral gorilla's life was a tragic battle over name, image and likeness — including full videos that the Cincinnati Zoo worked to hide... until now. Desus Nice and Katie Nolan help Pablo Torre get to the bottom of what gorillas in our midst can teach us about ourselves.
• Subscribe to Pablo's newsletter for exclusive access, documents and invites: https://www.pablo.show/
• Subscribe to "Casuals with Katie Nolan": https://www.youtube.com/katienolan

entry.title: How to Win at Jeopardy! (and at Life) with Mina Kimes | PTFO
entry.summary:
After winning "Celebrity Jeopardy!" All-Stars 2026, Mina Kimes shares her strategy of overpreparedness with Pablo Torre and Domonique Foxworth: How is studying for a game show like playing in the NFL? What's the first rule of "Jeopardy!" Fight Club? And why would you bet zero dollars with a million on the line? Plus: Katie Nolan's secret pep talk, Daily Double brain glitch, Spygate... and a buzzer bibliography.

entry.title: Jared McCain Goes Off, Grading The 25-26 Sixers, Jameer Nelson's President Candidacy

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary: Jared McCain is playing a lot for the Thunder, including a 24 point performance in OKC's Game 3 win. We talk about how McCain looks and rehash the trade, give out grades for almost all of the current Sixers, and talk about the report that Jameer Nelson is an internal candidate for the Sixers open President of Basketball Operations position. entry.title: Unlocking Secrets of Dianna Russini, Jordon Hudson... and bin Laden? | PTFO
entry.summary:
He dug up Dianna Russini's playlist for Mike Vrabel. And Osama bin Laden's p*rn stash from the CIA. He even got a DM from Bill Belichick's girlfriend on the prowl. Now, FOIAball's David Covucci is shattering the illusion of coaches' power — and the unfathomable pastimes of the billionaire booster class — one embarrassing document bomb at a time. Pablo Torre finds a kindred spirit... and Jessica Smetana makes history.

entry.title: How Billionaires Ate Fans Alive, with Mike Schur & Joe Posnanski | PTFO
entry.summary:
Have billionaire owners left us in the twilight of American fandom? Are crying Liverpool fans actually the cure for male loneliness? And is the NFL offseason running out of juice? Or would you get married during the Super Bowl on Valentine's Day? Plus: Jim Thome's farm adjacency, Regis Philbin analytics, Gerry and the Pacemakers, institutional investors, the worst pandemic crowd... and feeding a sandwich to Stugotz on Microsoft Teams.
• Read "Big Fan": https://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/778607/big-fan-by-michael-schur-and-joe-posnanski-foreword-by-tom-hanks/

entry.title: A Deep Dive on Bob Myers With Two Bob Myers Experts

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
Bob Myers is either hiring the new President of Basketball Operations, doing the job, or both. We know Bob Myers ran the Warriors, but there's so much more to know. Joining us for this podcast as we go deeper into Myers are two people who have covered Myers for years. Host of the Light Years podcast, Saam Esfandiari, and Ethan Sherwood-Strauss, now of House of Strauss, formerly of The Athletic and ESPN.

entry.title: Debunking Oz Pearlman's Tricks: Is He a Fraud? | PTFO
entry.summary:
He's wowed presidents, pro athletes and podcasters. But magicians tell us Oz Pearlman's viral act has crossed an ethical line. Stevie Baskin, after obsessively studying Pearlman's tricks, explains to Pablo Torre how influencers from Charles Barkley to the White House were in on the act — and why he thinks this brand of "mind-reading" amounts to fraud.
• Watch much more from Stevie Baskin: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FnwcU-XDyJ4
• Previously on PTFO: The Unwritten Rules of Magic https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=J-efPrAa7i8
• Subscribe to Pablo's newsletter for exclusive access, documents and invites: https://www.pablo.show/
(Pablo Torre Finds Out is independently produced by Meadowlark Media and distributed by The Athletic. The views, research and reporting expressed in this episode are solely those of Pablo Torre Finds Out and do not reflect the work or editorial input of The Athletic or its journalists.)

(4:45) Seahawks: manageable schedule for the champs (11:05) Rams: holy $h!t hard start, holy $h!t hard finish (15:10) 49ers: soft return after Australia (17:40) Eagles: could Philly be a dumpster fire? (22:50) Bears: first time they’ll have a target on their back (26:50) Patriots: favorable schedule for a team that was just in the Super Bowl (30:10) Broncos: they hit the Holy Sh!t category, one of the toughest starts of any team (33:25) Chiefs: early BYE could be perfect timing (38:15) Bills: two stretches of 4 of 5 on the road will be tricky (43:15) DraftKings: New Head Coaches Week 1 Odds (45:10) Cardinals: Arizona could start 0-13 (48:10) Lions: should be 4-1 entering BYE week (49:55) Cowboys: early travel could get tricky despite them being better (51:30) Commanders: opening stretch is brutal (52:45) Ravens: early schedule sets up really nice for them (54:05) Jets: still not easy, but better than it’s been (56:55) Colts: could be 0-3 to start, and they need to make it happen this year (58:20) Jaguars: did Trevor Lawrence really cut his hair? entry.title: Decoding the NFL vs. Rupert Murdoch (and TV Networks vs. Big Tech) | PTFO
entry.summary:
What do the tech titans of Silicon Valley and the old heads of traditional broadcast television have in common? Answer: they all need to kiss the ring of Roger Goodell. But will the NFL get too greedy in their continued pursuit of maximum return on their media rights? And why is Rupert Murdoch secretly meeting with Donald Trump? The Sporting Class — aka “Rich Guys OnlyFans” — returns…
Read Vanity Fair's "Roger Goodell's Hollywood Blitz: Why All the Other Moguls Have to Kiss the Ring" https://www.vanityfair.com/culture/story/roger-goodells-hollywood-blitz?srsltid=AfmBOorbSwEq_gn5FPDfzAy0jLtH6AviWhEdPSD6lGboChG4S5UhlT-g
Read Wall Street Journal's "Rupert Murdoch’s High-Stakes Blitz Against the NFL" https://www.wsj.com/business/media/murdoch-nfl-television-fight-35aa3587
Subscribe to "Nothing Personal with David Samson" https://www.youtube.com/@NPDS

entry.title: EXTENDED BEST-OF EPISODE: Favorite show moments and guests | Fantasy Football Happy Hour (FULL)
entry.summary:
Matthew (@MatthewBerryTMR) Jay (@croucherJD) and Connor (@ConnorJRogers) look back on the most outstanding moments over the last four years of the Fantasy Football Happy and are joined by a bevy of show contributors both in front and behind the scenes, including Pat Daugherty (@RotoPat), Denny Carter (@CDCarter13), and Lawrence (@LordDontLose). They also spend a little extra time in the discord with the Regulars answering questions and continuing long-standing show themes. #NFL #NBCSports #happyhour
Chapters: (2:30) – Matthew gives an update on the show (4:40) – Best moments from the last four years of the show (16:45) – RotoPat and Denny Carter join the show (27:30) – Penn State Blake and Big Show drop by (36:35) – Best of Fantasy Football Pregame (42:50) – Backup Producer Pete stops by the set (45:10) – The Regulars share their shoutouts (48:20) – Lawrence Jackson joins the guys in studio (52:10) – Best of Super Bowl Radio Row guests (58:10) – Extended discord conversations with the Regulars

entry.title: The Demise (and Future) of LIV Golf, Explained with Alan Shipnuck | PTFO
entry.summary:
Donald Trump may have ended his favorite sport's civil war — by waging war on Iran. But what did we really learn from Saudi Arabia's multibillion-dollar LIV Golf experiment? Journalist Alan Shipnuck explains what insiders are texting about but won't say out loud: that golfers are the most overpaid athletes on Earth, that peacetime is suddenly more complicated for the PGA Tour... and that sportswashing is insidious because it works.
• Read "LIV and Let Die" by Alan Shipnuck: https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/LIV-and-Let-Die/Alan-Shipnuck/9781668020012
• Previously on PTFO: Phil Mickelson, Trump and the "Big Daddy" Group-Chat Leak https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UbmBZR5aoRY
• Plus: Trump, Golf, and Saudi Arabia's Deal https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=e0o4KKO-fdE
entry.content:
Donald Trump may have ended his favorite sport's civil war — by waging war on Iran. But what did we really learn from Saudi Arabia's multibillion-dollar LIV Golf experiment? Journalist Alan Shipnuck explains what insiders are texting about but won't say out loud: that golfers are the most overpaid athletes on Earth, that peacetime is suddenly more complicated for the PGA Tour... and that sportswashing is insidious because it works.

• Read "LIV and Let Die" by Alan Shipnuck:
https://www.simonandschuster.com/books/LIV-and-Let-Die/Alan-Shipnuck/9781668020012

• Previously on PTFO: Phil Mickelson, Trump and the "Big Daddy" Group-Chat Leak
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UbmBZR5aoRY

• Plus: Trump, Golf, and Saudi Arabia's Deal
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=e0o4KKO-fdE

entry.title: Decrypting New MSG Surveillance Scoops & Whistleblower Power with ACLU's Ben Wizner | PTFO
entry.summary:
The Knicks are surging. But politicians are condemning owner James Dolan and his tax breaks, after our investigation with WIRED. So Pablo Torre tests the limits of facial recognition with his playoff ticket plug Ben Wizner, who just happens to be the Deputy Legal Director at the ACLU — and one of America's foremost experts on modern privacy. Which makes him uniquely (if begrudgingly) qualified to break down everything from spooked NBA journalists and a banned puppy, to Jeffrey Epstein and Harvey Weinstein connections… to, yes, the Mount Rushmore of whistleblowers.
• Previously on PTFO: Watching Fans, Monitoring Kids, Tracking Players — Knicks Surveillance, Revealed https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yNZ3hJPN5Vc
• From WIRED: "The Shocking Secrets of Madison Square Garden’s Surveillance Machine" https://www.wired.com/story/madison-square-garden-jim-dolan-surveillance-machine/
• PTFO Vault: Thunder GM Sam Presti's Jazz-Rap Album, Unearthed https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TGFpdy6upiU
(Pablo Torre Finds Out is independently produced by Meadowlark Media and distributed by The Athletic. The views, research and reporting expressed in this episode are solely those of Pablo Torre Finds Out and do not reflect the work or editorial input of The Athletic or its journalists.)

entry.title: 2026 Rookie Positional Rankings: Jeremiyah Love, Price, Tate | Fantasy Football Happy Hour (FULL)
entry.summary:
Matthew (@MatthewBerryTMR) details his time at the Kentucky Derby which included a strange interaction at dinner to Jay (@croucherJD) and Connor (@ConnorJRogers), then the trio dive into where Matthew is placing rookies in his positional rankings this season. Jeremiyah Love, Jordyn Tyson, and Fernando Mendoza are among the players discussed. The crew also highlights A.J. Brown, Jaxson Dart and other veteran movers in the rankings along the way and wrap up the show with their favorite bets to win Super Bowl LXI. #NBCSports #FantasyFootballHappyHour #NFL
Chapters: (0:00) - Welcome (1:45) – Matthew recaps his time betting Churchill Downs and shares an unusual story going out to dinner (7:30) – Rookie RB Rankings: Jeremiyah Love (RB11), Jadarian Price (RB27) (18:45) – Rookie WR Rankings: Carnell Tate (WR25), Jordyn Tyson (WR32), Makai Lemon (WR44), KC Concepcion (WR46), Omar Cooper Jr. (WR55) + Germie Bernard, Antonio Williams intrigue (31:40) – Notable Veteran WR Movers: A.J. Brown (WR11), DeVonta Smith (WR14), Terry McLaurin (WR22) (37:20) – Jay reveals why he is feverishly rooting for Arsenal to win the Premier League (40:50) – Rookie QB Rankings: Fernando Mendoza (QB28) + Jaxson Dart (QB9), Tyler Shough (QB17) among top veteran movers (44:40) – Rookie TE Rankings: Kenyon Sadiq (TE20) + Mark Andrews (TE12), Dallas Goedert (TE13) headline veteran shifts (50:05) – Last Call: Way too early Super Bowl LXI Champion bets

entry.title: Simms goes to London + 2026 Rookie Predictions | Chris Simms Unbuttoned (FULL Ep. 868) | NFL on NBC
entry.summary:
Welcome from London! Chris is across the pond enjoying his time learning Arsenal chants & drinking outside pubs, while Connor is at home…trying to grow something on his face? The guys do some Rookie Predictions and try to find this year’s Tyler Shough, Tetairoa McMillan, and more. Then, they go through some NFL news on Patrick Mahomes & Fernando Mendoza, before critiquing Malik Wills’ viral first pitch at a Marlins game. Plus, we take an impromptu poll of which Simms impression is better: Patrick Mahomes or Bane? #NFLonNBC #NFL #NFLDraft
Chapters: (0:00) “What do you have growing on your face?” (7:10) Chris learned an Arsenal chant (14:05) RIP Yankees legend John Sterling (16:25) 2026 Rookie Predictions: This year’s Cam Ward? Raiders QB Fernando Mendoza (20:35) This year’s Tyler Shough? Cardinals QB Carson Beck (24:20) This year’s TreVeyon Henderson? Cardinals RB Jeremiyah Love & Seahawks RB Jadarian Price (27:25) This year’s Quinshon Judkins? Commanders RB Kaytron Allen & Broncos RB Jonah Coleman (30:00) This year’s Cam Skattebo? Chargers WR Brenen Thompson & Buccaneers DB Keionte Scott (32:45) This year’s Tetairoa McMillan? Titans WR Carnell Tate, Saints WR Jordyn Tyson, Commanders WR Antonio Williams (37:05) This year’s Luther Burden III? Panthers WR Chris Brazzell II & Dolphins WR Chris Bell (39:40) This year’s Grey Zabel? Texans OL Keylan Rutledge & Ravens OL Vega Ioane (41:00) This year’s James Pearce Jr? Jets DE David Bailey, LB Malachi Lawrence, 49ers DL Romello Height (44:00) This year’s Abdul Carter? Chiefs DE R Mason Thomas, Ravens DE Zion Young, Patriots LB Gabe Jacas (47:15) This year’s Carson Schwesinger? entry.content:
Welcome from London! Chris is across the pond enjoying his time learning Arsenal chants & drinking outside pubs, while Connor is at home…trying to grow something on his face? The guys do some Rookie Predictions and try to find this year’s Tyler Shough, Tetairoa McMillan, and more. Then, they go through some NFL news on Patrick Mahomes & Fernando Mendoza, before critiquing Malik Wills’ viral first pitch at a Marlins game. Plus, we take an impromptu poll of which Simms impression is better: Patrick Mahomes or Bane? #NFLonNBC #NFL #NFLDraft

entry.title: Post NFL Draft Love/Hate live from Churchill Downs | Happy Hour (FULL)
entry.summary:
Live from Churchill Downs, Matthew (@MatthewBerryTMR), Lawrence (@LordDontLose) and Drew Dinsick (@whale_capper) discuss Matthew’s Love/Hate list following the NFL Draft, highlighting Jalen Hurts, Cam Skattebo, Jadarian Price, DeVonta Smith, and Jordyn Tyson among others. Steve Kornacki (@SteveKornacki) also stops by to highlight favorites and long shot wagers he’s making not only for the Kentucky Derby, but also Friday’s Kentucky Oaks. #NBCSports #FantasyFootballHappyHour #NFL

entry.title: Beijing Auto Show 2026: 8 GIANT SUVs. INSANE!
entry.summary:
At Beijing Auto Show 2026, over 1,400 cars were unveiled — but we're focusing on the 8 BIGGEST Chinese SUVs that are rewriting the rulebook. From a $22,000 budget giant to a $77,000 luxury flagship that makes Range Rover look like poverty spec, China is building some of the most impressive large SUVs on the planet.In this video, we walk you through the best massive SUVs from the Beijing Auto Show 2026 — covering design, tech, range, and price. Whether you're into off-road monsters, tech-loaded flagships, or incredible value, there's something here that will blow your mind.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ____________________________________________________________ ✅ Highlights:
Wuling Xingguang L — 6-seater under $22,000 with 260km EV range Nio ES9 — $77,000 flagship with 42-point massage & battery swap in 3 minutes Freelander — British name, Chinese tech, 5.1 meters of modern muscle Hongqi 1927 — 4 motors, 1,850 Nm, three locking diffs & 0-100 in under 4 seconds VW Unix 08 — Built on XPENG's G9 platform with VLA 2.0 ADAS & 700km range XPENG GX — 5.3 meters, 3000 TOPS compute, 750km range — the tech king Leapmotor D19 — Longer than BMW X7, starts at $32,000 — the value king BYD Grand Tang — 780hp, megawatt flash charging, 0-97% in 8 minutes
____________________________________________________________ ⏱️ Video Chapters:
00:00 - Intro: Beijing Auto Show 2026
Top 8 BIG SUVs 00:32 - Wuling Xingguan L 02:14 - NIO ES9 03:18 - FREELANDER 97 Concept 05:11 - Hongqi 1927 07:09 - VW UNYX 08 08:45 - Xpeng GX 10:39 - Leapmotor D19 12:25 - BYD Da Tang
Honourable Mentions 13:49 - Beijing CITY HUNTER 14:08 - VW ID. ERA 9X 14:38 - Li Auto L9 Livis
15:13 - OUTRO

entry.title: NIO ONVO L80 is Perfectly BORING with MASSIVE storage!
entry.summary:
The ONVO L80 is the latest EV from NIO's sub brand. The ONVO L80 is a 5-seat variant of the previously popular ONVO L90. In this video, we take a quick yet thorough look at the L80 and explore how ONVO has reworked the L90 into a large 5 seat version.
This video covers all the major details of the ONVO L80, from its stylish green colour and frunk features to the massive trunk space that easily rivals the NIO ET5 Touring. We also take a look at the upgraded ADAS system, now with roof-mounted LIDAR, making the ONVO L80 an even smarter vehicle than the first ONVO L90.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ____________________________________________________________ ✅ Highlights:
Same dimensions as the L90 with a new green color Spacious 240-liter frunk & innovative camp kitchen Upgraded ADAS hardware, including roof-mounted LIDAR Impressive 17.3-inch rear entertainment screen 23-speaker sound system with 7.1.4 Dolby Atmos Efficient with 14.3kWh/100km for rear-wheel drive
____________________________________________________________ ⏱️ Video Chapters:

They also discuss Makai Lemon, Jadarian Price, and Kenyon Sadiq among others before highlighting their favorite Day 2 and 3 selections. The show concludes with the crew’s best bets for OROY and DROY. #NBCSports #FantasyFootballHappyHour #NFL

entry.summary:
Matthew (@MatthewBerryTMR) updates Jay (@croucherJD), Connor (@ConnorJRogers), and Lawrence (@LordDontLose) on his recent vacation and the guys explain why Connor drinks free before dialing into Matthew’s way too early rankings for WRs and TEs in 2026. Justin Jefferson, Zay Flowers, and Ladd McConkey headlined the WR discussions while Colston Loveland, Tucker Kraft, and George Kittle are featured among TEs. To wrap up the show, the crew unveils their favorite bets for first and second WR to be drafted next week. #NBCSports #FantasyFootballHappyHour #NFL

entry.title: The Sixers Are In The Postseason, What Do We Want Out Of It?

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary: The Sixers beat the Bucks, and the Magic lost to the backup Celtics, so the Sixers will host the play-in game on Wednesday in Philadelphia. We talk about what we’re hoping to get out of the play-in and playoffs, more on the Embiid and his appendicitis, an amazing clip of Michael Rubin talking about the White Party, and Mike gets to hear the new Doc Rivers song as he gets fired. entry.title: Joel Embiid's Appendix Surgery, Sixers Lose To The Rockets, Doc Rivers Is Full Of It (Again)

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
Joel Embiid has had many different injuries for the Sixers, but this is his first appendicitis. Joel Embiid had surgery on Thursday, just hours before the game against the Rockets. The Sixers will likely be without him for the play in and first round of the playoffs, if they make it. We talk about the situation, and the loss to the Rockets.

entry.title: NIO ES9: Germany Has NO ANSWER To This Chinese SUV!
entry.summary:
The NIO ES9 is the SUV version of NIO's flagship ET9 sedan, combining high-tech luxury with the space and versatility of a large SUV. With its commanding road presence, 6 seat configuration, and a tech-forward interior, it might just be the answer to the growing demand for luxury EV SUVs. But how does it perform? Does it live up to the luxury SUV competition, or is it just another overhyped tech showcase?
In this video, we explore the NIO ES9’s design, features. Do you really need to spend top dollar for a luxury Rolls, Bentley or Aston to get the best of the best? I don't think so.
Highlights and Video Chapters below 👇 ______________________________________________________________
✅ Highlights:
- Stunning road presence - 600L trunk space - Brand new 47-speaker sound system with Dolby Atmos - Instant intelligent privacy glass - 42-point Spa Massage - 20-point foot massage! ______________________________________________________________
⏱️ Video Chapters:
00:00 - NIO ES9 Intro 00:35 - NIO Recap 03:12 - Exterior Features: Headlights, Frunk, and More 01:25 - Why will the NIO ES9 sell better? 01:59 - NIO ES9 vs Competition
02:49 - NIO ES9 Exterior Styling 03:11 - NIO ES9 Frunk Space 04:12 - NIO ES9 Exterior Styling Continued

entry.content:
The NIO ES9 is the SUV version of NIO's flagship ET9 sedan, combining high-tech luxury with the space and versatility of a large SUV. With its commanding road presence, 6 seat configuration, and a tech-forward interior, it might just be the answer to the growing demand for luxury EV SUVs. But how does it perform? Does it live up to the luxury SUV competition, or is it just another overhyped tech showcase?

In this video, we explore the NIO ES9’s design, features. Do you really need to spend top dollar for a luxury Rolls, Bentley or Aston to get the best of the best? I don't think so.

entry.title: Paul George Is Cookin', Sixers Defense and Rebounding, Drummond's Corner Threes

We discuss Paul George's play since his return, the Sixers issues on defense and rebounding, potentially playing Adem Bona with Embiid, and Andre Drummond's corner threes. Then we tell Adam how to hide in the Sixers film sessions, talk about what Evan Turner's problem is, and congratulate Doc Rivers.

entry.title: Embiid and Paul George Are Back... And Maybe The Sixers Are Too

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

We talk about how both players looked, what it means for the rest of the season, as well as VJ Edgecombe plowing through the rookie wall, and whether Justin Edwards will stay in the rotation. Then we discuss Nick Nurse's comments about Jared McCain, and all of those babies whining about their precious 65 game rule.

entry.title: Reaching The Zugzwang, Connie And Jack, Barely Beating Nets

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
On today's show, Spike and Mike discuss MOC’s article on if the Sixers have reached the Zugzwang, referenced in the Hinkie letter, that describes the situation where you’re forced to make a move that will worsen your position. MOC proposes that the Sixers have three main options moving forward. Mike is joined later on by Bo Belanger, creator of Connie and Jack: A Rigged Game, a podcast that follows Connie Hawkins, basketball superstar who is blackballed from the NBA for a crime he didn’t commit. Oh, and the Sixers barely beat the Nets.
MOC article: https://www.rightstorickysanchez.com/p/have-we-reached-the-zugzwang Connie and Jack: https://www.connieandjackpod.com/

entry.title: Live Ricky From New Orleans featuring Jake Fischer, Amos Lee, and Mia Kang

author.name: Rights To Ricky Sanchez: The Sixers Podcast

entry.summary:
The game at Fly The Process was a disaster, but the trip was amazing. We did the pod live from the legendary Preservation Hall in New Orleans, with special guests Jake Fischer, Amos Lee, and our official poet Mia Kang. We talked about the team's struggles, Morey, Nurse, the team's future and the guy who got his identity stolen on Bourbon Street when he was drunk.
